instructions.instruction: Before creating any file, check REVIEWED.md and references/REFERENCE_INDEX.md for the arxiv ID or slug. If already present, report the duplicate and stop.
instructions.relevant_when: User provides a paper to triage.
instructions.why_given: The skill mandates a duplicate check as step 2, and the NEVER anti-pattern prohibits creating a new references file without checking first.

instructions.instruction: Derive a stable slug in the form <firstauthor-surname>-<2-3-word-topic> (e.g. jiang-llmlingua, press-longchat).
instructions.relevant_when: Creating the references file and REVIEWED.md entry for any paper.
instructions.why_given: The skill specifies this naming pattern explicitly in step 1 to ensure consistent, collision-resistant identifiers.

instructions.instruction: Always mark benchmark and evaluation results as '(as reported)' — never state metrics as verified facts.
instructions.relevant_when: Populating the Evaluation section of references/<slug>.md.
instructions.why_given: The NEVER anti-pattern prohibits inventing or presenting benchmark numbers as verified; the skill requires the '(as reported)' qualifier on all metrics.

instructions.instruction: Create references/<slug>.md using templates/REFERENCE-paper.md. Populate every section; mark inapplicable subsections as N/A rather than leaving them blank.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing the reference summary for any newly triaged paper.
instructions.why_given: The skill specifies this template as the canonical source for structure and requires every section to be addressed.

instructions.instruction: Add a reverse-chronological row to the REVIEWED.md summary table with columns: date, slug, type (paper), disposition (pending), one-line description.
instructions.relevant_when: After completing the reference summary for any paper.
instructions.why_given: Step 5 of the workflow requires updating REVIEWED.md with a table row and a detailed section.

instructions.instruction: Add a detailed section to REVIEWED.md below the summary table including arxiv ID, authors, date, tags, 2-3 sentence summary, and disposition 'pending'.
instructions.relevant_when: After completing the reference summary for any paper.
instructions.why_given: Step 5 specifies the exact fields required in the detailed REVIEWED.md section.

instructions.instruction: Do not create an ANALYSIS-*.md file during triage. Offer promotion as an option to the user after the triage is complete.
instructions.relevant_when: At the end of any triage workflow.
instructions.why_given: The NEVER anti-pattern explicitly prohibits creating ANALYSIS-*.md as part of triage; the skill requires the user to confirm promotion.

instructions.instruction: Add a row to references/REFERENCE_INDEX.md under the most relevant topic category table.
instructions.relevant_when: After completing the reference summary and REVIEWED.md entry.
instructions.why_given: Step 6 requires updating the REFERENCE_INDEX.md as part of every triage.
